    4. The Existential Crisis Checklist
</h2>

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>And h<span style="color: var(--squid-ink);">ere you are again.</span></p><p><span style="color: var(--squid-ink);">In this familiar place: Your reccuring cycle of doubt.</span></p><p>Who put you in this state this time?</p><p>What happened in your life to cause this?</p><p><em>Trick question.</em></p><p>You know it has nothing to do with "them" or the outside world.</p><p>How you see the world is up to you.</p><p>And you're seeing it in a negative way right now.</p><p>Could it be because you...</p><p>You feel like you've stopped growing?</p><p>An artist that's not growing feels lackluster. </p><p>Like somethings off.</p><p>Like they're not living to their full potential.</p><p>And it's sneaky. It creeps up on you.</p><p>You may not notice it at first. Especially if you've been blending in with the "normals" who make a habit out of living like this no problem.</p><p>But for an artist... "Phoning it in" kills us.</p><p>The reason you feel bad / lost / uncentered isn't because of one bad thing that happened, or because you woke up on the wrong side of bed today...</p><p>The feelings you have right now are the result of an accumulation of little things that you stopped doing.</p><p>Somewhere along the way, maybe you got cocky.</p><p>You started believing the Hallmark Cards.</p><p>And you stopped doing the things that brought you to your power in the first place.</p><p>Despite the fact that me and everyone else around you knows you're amazing... YOU totally forgot.</p><p>And why?</p><p>Because youu stopped doing the things that reinforce your gifts. </p><p>And now you feel "lesser than". </p><p>From now on... The moment you feel bad, let it trigger a part of your brain that asks:</p><p><em>"What did I stop doing that allowed this feeling to creep up on me again?"</em></p><p>Here is a checklist of questions for you to come back to every time you feel like it's all going to shit:</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>1. Are you doing your daily practice? Are you CREATING every day?</strong></h1><h1><strong>2. Are you staying off social media and other mind-numbing distractions? Especially in the morning. Especially at night.</strong></h1><h1>
<strong>3. </strong><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/30093395" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"><strong>Are you journaling?</strong></a><strong> (This is too basic. It's crazy.) Have you read back the last few weeks to see if your current angst isn't all in your head? Are you falling in any noticeable pattern?</strong>
</h1><h1>
<strong>4. Are you suffering from </strong><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/23650999" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"><strong>"Complication Creep"</strong></a><strong>? Is it time to simplify your goals back to their essence?</strong>
</h1><h1><strong>5. Did you write a note to yourself when you were feeling good -- so that you could re-read it in times like these, to remind yourself what your priorities are?</strong></h1><h1><strong>6. Have you created an environment for yourself with multiple cues to remind you of your goals? Have you "refreshed them" so that they don't blend into the background (and the recesses of your mind).</strong></h1><h1><strong>7. Are you hanging out with positive, vibrant people?</strong></h1><h1><strong>8. Are you stretching, breathing, working out, meditating?</strong></h1><h1><strong>9. Are you getting enough sleep?</strong></h1><p><br></p><p>If you aren't doing these things (the energy builders), why would you expect to be feeling good right now?</p><p>You're an artist. You need to re-align with artist-on-the-rise behaviors.</p><p>If you don't feel like immediately correcting the things you're missing above ask yourself:</p><p><strong>Do I love the angst?</strong></p><p><strong>Do I love the drama?</strong></p><p><strong>Do I love the emotional distress?</strong></p><p><strong>Does it make me feel "alive"?</strong></p><p><strong>If so... Am I using it at least to create something incredible?</strong></p><p>The fact that you're reading this is promising.</p><p>Live up to that promise with what you decide to do next.</p><p><em>Your audience is waiting for you to get back to the mission.</em></p><p></p>
</div>
